diagnosed: Research agent workflow fails ~50% of runs (7 of the last ~25) at the 'Fail loudly if neither model produced work' step. Triaged: quota is green (session 15%, week 8%), and the failing runs show the agent running full turns at real cost (is_error:false, 17-19 turns, $2.50-$7.79) yet producing no commit. Since the workflow stages research-log.json in `git add` and HEAD stays unchanged, the agent is NOT writing research-log.json on 'found nothing new' runs. No write => no commit => the workflow misreads it as total quota exhaustion and exits 1.
fixed: scripts/agents/research.md: made research-log.json an unconditional always-write (even a no-op run with eventsAdded:0), with the why (no-commit is read as quota exhaustion) so a genuine run always commits and 'no commit' reliably means the agent never ran.
notes: Not touched (deliberately): research-agent.yml (protected .github/workflows/**) — the prompt fix is the root cause and lives in a non-protected, auto-mergeable path.

Ignored as non-bugs: Deploy Pages failures ('Deployment failed, try again later' = transient GitHub infra); Static pipeline failure (its deploy sub-job, same transient Pages issue); Coverage-critic 29001983640 (conclusion: cancelled via concurrency, not a failure); Follow-request 28860312429 ('GitHub Actions is not permitted to create or approve pull requests' = a repo setting, not code, one-off).

Verified healthy: npm test (261 pass), validate-events.js (0 errors), all sport data files parse, and every deterministic pipeline script (build-events, build-ics, detect-coverage-gaps, aggregate-calibration) runs clean.

Proof limitation: the end-to-end effect depends on the research agent's runtime behavior and can't be reproduced in CI; the coherence gate (tests/agent-prompts.test.js) and full npm test pass, confirming the contract stays intact and nothing regresses.
